title: Amar Ghumontovilla single-page literary portal (sections, comments, contact, translate, SEO, floral theme)

requirements.summary: Implement a single-page app shell with top navigation that switches between Home/Poems/Stories/Essays/Contact sections and highlights the active section.
acceptanceCriteria: A header nav is present on all views with links for Home/Poems/Stories/Essays/Contact.
Clicking a nav item shows the corresponding section content and hides the others without refreshing the page.
The currently selected section is clearly highlighted.

file_operations.description: Create the main single-page layout with a sticky/top header navigation, internal section switching (React state), and active-section styling. Use shadcn-ui primitives (e.g., Button) where appropriate for consistent nav interaction; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a dedicated TopNav component that renders the five section links and applies an active state based on the current section. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Button) for accessible interactive controls; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Ensure the app renders the new App component entry (and any providers needed for section state/hooks) without adding page reload navigation.

requirements.summary: Add a consistent responsive hero banner for each section with a floral image and the section title (hero image + heading structure).
acceptanceCriteria: Each section starts with a hero area that includes a static floral image and a title.
The hero layout is consistent across sections and responsive on mobile and desktop.

file_operations.description: Create a reusable SectionHero component that renders the generated floral hero image plus section title (and optional subtitle) with consistent spacing and responsive behavior. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire SectionHero into each section (Home/Poems/Stories/Essays/Contact) so the hero appears at the top of each section consistently.

requirements.summary: Implement per-section comments UI for Home/Poems/Stories/Essays with form + list, using backend-backed loading and submission with validation.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the four sections (Home/Poems/Stories/Essays) shows a comments area with a form and a list.
Submitting a comment adds it to the correct section list immediately and persists it in the canister.
Reloading the app shows previously submitted comments in the correct section.
Basic validation prevents submitting empty comments.

file_operations.description: Create a reusable comments component (textarea + submit + list) parameterized by section, with empty-comment validation, optimistic UI update, and error handling. Use shadcn-ui form primitives (e.g., Textarea, Button, Card) for consistent styling; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add a React Query-based hook to load and submit comments per section by calling the existing backend interface methods (get*Comments/add*Comment) and keeping cached lists in sync.

Provide a typed frontend backend client wrapper that exposes the existing backend interface to the UI (used by comments/contact hooks), aligning with the project's core-infrastructure connection patterns.
Render CommentsSection within Home/Poems/Stories/Essays sections (and not in Contact) and pass the correct section key so each section reads/writes its own persisted comments.

requirements.summary: Implement Contact section with email display and a validated Name/Email/Message form that persists submissions via backend and shows a clear success state.
acceptanceCriteria: The Contact section displays the email address exactly as provided.
The contact form validates required fields and basic email format.
Submitting the form stores the message in the backend and shows a success confirmation in the UI.
The form is cleared (or disabled) after a successful send to prevent duplicate submissions by mistake.

file_operations.description: Create the Contact section UI: render the email address (contact@amarghumontovilla.in) and a form with Name/Email/Message + Send button, including required-field checks and basic email format validation. Use shadcn-ui Input/Textarea/Button and optionally Alert for success messaging; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Implement submission logic (React Query mutation) calling addContactMessage, handling pending/success/error states, and clearing/disabling the form on success.
Wire ContactSection into the Contact section panel so it is shown when Contact is active and hidden otherwise.

requirements.summary: Add a Google Translate widget container and client-side script loader that renders the widget when available without breaking if it fails.
acceptanceCriteria: A "google_translate_element" container exists in the rendered app.
The Google Translate script is loaded on the client and the widget renders (when accessible) without breaking the app if it fails to load.

file_operations.description: Create a TranslateWidget component that renders a container with id "google_translate_element" and injects the Google Translate element script on the client with guarded initialization and failure-safe behavior.
Place TranslateWidget in the app chrome (e.g., header area) so it is present in the rendered UI without introducing additional routes/pages.

requirements.summary: Configure SEO and social metadata: title, meta description/keywords/author, Open Graph tags, and canonical URL for https://www.amarghumontovilla.in.
acceptanceCriteria: The built app sets the document title and includes meta tags for description/keywords/author.
Open Graph tags for title/description/image/url are present.
A canonical link tag exists for https://www.amarghumontovilla.in.

file_operations.description: Add/ensure meta tags for description, keywords, author; Open Graph tags (title/description/image/url); and canonical link for https://www.amarghumontovilla.in. Set og:image to the deployed static asset URL corresponding to the generated hero image (served from the public assets).

requirements.summary: Apply a calm, nature-inspired floral theme (palette, typography, spacing, buttons/forms) consistently across navigation, hero, content, and forms with good readability.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI has a consistent floral/nature-inspired theme and readable typography for long text.
Forms, buttons, navigation, and section layouts share consistent styling.
The theme works well on mobile and desktop and maintains good text contrast.

file_operations.description: Establish global theme tokens (CSS variables compatible with the existing Tailwind/shadcn-ui setup), base typography for long-form reading, and global layout/background styling that supports the floral aesthetic.
Extend Tailwind theme configuration (typography, colors, spacing as needed) to support the editorial floral theme consistently across components.

Refine navigation styling for the floral theme (active highlight, spacing, responsiveness) while maintaining accessibility. If using shadcn-ui components, verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Align comments UI (form fields, buttons, list typography) with the global floral editorial theme. If using shadcn-ui components, verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Align contact form controls and success state styling with the global floral editorial theme. If using shadcn-ui components, verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add required generated static images under frontend/public/assets/generated and use them for the hero image and optional background pattern; avoid external image hotlinking.
acceptanceCriteria: Newly generated image assets exist under frontend/public/assets/generated.
The hero banner uses the generated floral image from local static assets.
OG image metadata points to the correct deployed static image path (not a placeholder).

file_operations.description: Add the generated floral bouquet hero image asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/flower-bouquet-hero.dim_1920x800.png for local usage in the UI.
Add the generated subtle seamless floral pattern background asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/floral-pattern-bg.dim_1024x1024.png for optional global background usage.
Update SectionHero to reference the local static hero image at frontend/public/assets/generated/flower-bouquet-hero.dim_1920x800.png (not an external URL).
Apply the local background pattern at frontend/public/assets/generated/floral-pattern-bg.dim_1024x1024.png as an optional subtle page background (low contrast) consistent with the floral theme.

Ensure OG image metadata references the deployed URL corresponding to the local generated hero image (matching frontend/public/assets/generated/flower-bouquet-hero.dim_1920x800.png) rather than hotlinking external images.
